channel.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
channel.description:
On a fresh install, I'm getting the following errors on both wp.com and hosted WP JSON. This only happens on actual posts containing an image, so everything else seems to work so far.
Including a picture instead because the code editor here was insane.
![image|625x500](upload://bGnhOnNR8ceLcrZF411zRkPlxOU.png)
Any idea why this happens? Did I miss something? :)

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Ohhh, I see. Then we thought it was not working because in "auto" the browser is the one making the decision. So, if it works with "lazy", then it is working just fine! Maybe if we try with Slow 3G it does the lazy loading.

I’d leave the default as "lazy" for now…

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: David
item.description:
It seems like if you set loading="auto" even with the lazy image loading flag enabled in chrome://flags the images are not loaded lazily. If I set loading="lazy" for every Image, it works…

We can use loading="lazy" by default in the Image component or pass the loading property every time Image is rendered in the theme. We can also make it configurable from the settings and set it to lazy by default…

What would be the best option, @luisherranz?

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: David
item.description:
I see. I have been able to reproduce the bug and I’m working to solve it.

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
It doesn’t crash thanks to the latest changes, but native lazy load still doesn’t work! When the flag is active all the images are loaded.

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: David
item.description:
@orballo fixed the Illegal invocation error in this PR
It’s merged and released: Frontity update: 12 August 2019

The lazy image loading flag can now be enabled after doing a npm update in your Frontity project!

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
David:

@luisherranz, did you investigate anything about this flag?

It is the native lazy load flag.

We need to make it work because it will be enabled by default in Chrome 77.

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: David
item.description:
Yesterday, after my Chrome browser had updated to Version 76.0.3809.100 I started to get the same error. I disabled the Enable lazy image loading flag in chrome://flags (now it comes enabled by default) and then it worked as expected.

I think It wouldn’t be a good idea to ask users to deactivate it in order to use Frontity, it’s necessary to make Frontity compatible with that flag so I will investigate to see what can be done to fix this issue. @luisherranz, did you investigate anything about this flag?

In the meantime, the Enable lazy image loading flag should be disabled while developing with Frontity.

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Hey @espidesigns, I’m glad you solved it. Sorry for the delay in my answer

I’m going to test those flags here to see if we can figure out what may have caused the issue.

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: espidesigns
item.description:
@luisherranz Good news. I’m now able to load mars.frontity.org and my local site in Chrome after I cleared my internet data and junk in Chrome. Whew!

Thanks for all your help. Cheers!

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: espidesigns
item.description:
luisherranz:

Are you using any flags? (chrome://flags/)

I had flags enabled for “Enable lazy image loading”, “Enable lazy frame loading” and “Allow invalid certificates for resources loaded from localhost”. But when I disabled them nothing happened. Even after I reset all the flags to default.

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
espidesigns:

I appreciate your time looking into this, Luis.

No problem. We’ll find the problem sooner or later

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
@frontity/components@1.1.7 is the latest version so that’s ok.

espidesigns:

Must be something in my Chrome settings

Are you using any flags? (chrome://flags/)

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: espidesigns
item.description:
Great.

What happens when you visit https://mars.frontity.org? Does it fail?

It fails in Chrome (normal, incognito, and new profile). Still works on other browsers.

Are you aware of something different in your system/configuration?

The system/config is not that too modified. Chrome extensions have no effect. It still doesn’t load after opening the site in Incognito mode or after creating a new profile. However, when accessing the site on another PC, it loads on their Chrome. Must be something in my Chrome settings. I’ll let you know.

What version of @frontity/components do you have installed?
– You can check it with npm ls @frontity/components .

C:\Users\Mark\Local Sites\healy-frontity>npm ls @frontity/components
healy-frontity@1.0.0 C:\Users\Mark\Local Sites\healy-frontity
+-- @frontity/html2react@1.1.7
| `-- @frontity/components@1.1.7
`-- @frontity/mars-theme@1.1.7 -> C:\Users\Mark\Local Sites\healy-frontity\packages\mars-theme
  `-- @frontity/components@1.1.7  deduped

I appreciate your time looking into this, Luis.

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
I’ve recreated your enviornment:

  • Chrome 75.0.3770
  • Node 8.9.3
  • Npm 6.8.0
  • OS: Windows 10
  • Exact same Frontity packages (fresh install)

and I can’t make it fail:

I’m not sure what else to try…

  • Are you aware of something different in your system/configuration?
  • What version of @frontity/components do you have installed?
    – You can check it with npm ls @frontity/components.
  • What happens when you visit https://mars.frontity.org? Does it fail?

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: espidesigns
item.description:
I just tried to recreate on another fresh install. I’m able to open the single posts on Firefox and IE.

The issue below only happens in Chrome (Version 75.0.3770.100) and with the White Screen of Death on single posts view.

The above error occurred in the <Image> component:

Moreover, in /mars-theme/src/components/post.js, removing the line

<libraries.html2react.Component html={post.content.rendered} />

loads the post in Chrome but without the content, of course.

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: espidesigns
item.description:
No, but when I do it’s still showing the same error.

Just in case it matters, I’m running frontity on my local server and using a WordPress API from a staging site.

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
It’s working fine here with Node 8.9.3 and npm 6.8.0.

Are you using "https://test.frontity.io/wp-json" in state.source.api?

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: espidesigns
item.description:
Hi @luisherranz. Here you go. Thanks

System:

  • OS: Windows 10
  • CPU: (8) x64 Intel® Core™ i7-7700 CPU @ 3.60GHz
  • Memory: 1.67 GB / 7.95 GB

Binaries:

  • Node: 8.9.3 - C:\Program Files\nodejs\node.EXE
  • npm: 6.8.0 - C:\Program Files\nodejs\npm.CMD

Browsers:

  • Edge: 42.17134.1.0
  • Internet Explorer: 11.0.17134.1

npmPackages:

  • @frontity/core: ^1.1.2 => 1.1.2
  • @frontity/html2react: ^1.1.7 => 1.1.7
  • @frontity/mars-theme: ./packages/mars-theme => 1.1.7
  • @frontity/tiny-router: ^1.0.11 => 1.0.11
  • @frontity/wp-source: ^1.1.7 => 1.1.7
  • frontity: ^1.2.1 => 1.2.1

npmGlobalPackages:

  • frontity: Not Found
  • npx: Not Found

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
We haven’t been able to reproduce the problem.

Can you please run npx frontity info and paste your information here so we can try to match your system configuration?

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: espidesigns
item.description:
Hi. I am also getting the same errors on a fresh install on my local server with a staging API. How was this resolved?

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Both APIs work fine here on a fresh install. Images are shown, no errors on console.

Could you please try again on a fresh install?

If it still happens, could you please run npx frontity info and paste your information here so we can try to match your system configuration?

Thanks in advance.

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: patrik
item.description:
Of course, thanks! Sending you a private message with the two examples

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
May I know the url of the API and the post that has the problem? I’ll take a look

Read full topic

item.title: Illegal invocation at image.tsx (1.0.6)
item.dc:creator: patrik
item.description:
On a fresh install, I’m getting the following errors on both wp.com and hosted WP JSON. This only happens on actual posts containing an image, so everything else seems to work so far.

Including a picture instead because the code editor here was insane.

Any idea why this happens? Did I miss something?

Read full topic
